name: Precious Metals Refinery
description_short: Precious metals and luxury goods buyer offering up to 94% of spot value for gold, silver, platinum, jewelry, watches, and coins at 9+ AZ and CA locations.
description_long:
Precious Metals Refinery (PMR) was founded in 2011 by Robert Chorost and David Schiesel, headquartered in Tucson, Arizona. The company operates as a direct buyer of precious metals and luxury goods, with nine or more retail locations across Arizona (Tucson, Scottsdale, Marana, Phoenix) and California (Palm Springs, Palm Desert, Indio, and San Diego/Mission Valley). PMR employs GIA-certified gemologists, numismatic experts, and luxury watch specialists, positioning itself as a specialist buyer rather than a generalist pawn operation. The Tucson LLC entity holds BBB accreditation as of March 2022, though accreditation status varies by location.

PMR purchases a wide range of assets directly from the public: gold of all karats, silver, platinum, and palladium; fine jewelry and loose diamonds; luxury watches from brands like Rolex, Cartier, and Patek Philippe; coins and bullion; sterling flatware; antiques; and full estates. Their published payout rates are 94% of spot value for gold and 90% of spot for silver, platinum, and palladium — with a $50 assay, melt, and treatment fee applied to all lots. Evaluations are free and no-obligation. For customers who cannot visit in person, PMR offers a nationwide mail-in service with free insured shipping kits. In-home consultations are available for large estate liquidations. The company also lists loans, jewelry repair, and watch repair among its services, though loan terms are not publicly detailed on their website.

PMR differentiates itself through transparent, in-person testing using a Thermo Fisher Scientific Niton XRF analyzer — a non-destructive elemental test performed in front of the customer, who is never separated from their items during evaluation. The company claims to pay up to 40% more than competitors and emphasizes same-day payment, private buying offices at every location, and strict transaction confidentiality. The combination of published spot-percentage payouts and specialist staff (gemologists, numismatists, watch experts) makes PMR more comparable to a specialty refinery than a typical pawn shop.

PMR's core strength is its published payout transparency and multi-category expertise — most gold buyers do not openly post spot percentages, making PMR's 94% gold rate a verifiable benchmark for comparison shopping. Its 5.0 Google rating across 83 reviews and BBB accreditation (Tucson entity) suggest a consistent customer experience. The primary limitation is structural: the $50 assay fee reduces net returns on smaller lots, and once a transaction is completed there is no recourse — this is a final sale, not a pawn with redemption rights. Loan terms, while listed as a service, are not publicly disclosed, which limits evaluation of that offering. Customers outside Arizona and California are limited to the mail-in service, which adds logistical steps compared to an in-person transaction.
address: 3111 Camino Del Rio N #400, San Diego, CA 92108

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee — all transactions are final sales. Free, no-obligation appraisals allow customers to walk away without selling. Confidentiality on all transactions is guaranteed.

tiers.name: Gold Purchases
features: 94% of current spot value paid for gold
All karats accepted (10K, 14K, 18K, 22K, 24K, dental gold, scrap)
In-person XRF elemental testing performed in front of customer

tiers.name: Silver, Platinum & Palladium Purchases
features: Coins, bars, bullion, and sterling flatware accepted

tiers.name: Jewelry, Watches & Specialty Items
features: GIA-certified gemologists evaluate diamonds and fine jewelry
Luxury watch specialists assess Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe, and more
Numismatic experts for coins and bullion
Free appraisals with no obligation to sell
Private buying offices at all locations

pros: Published 94% of spot value for gold — one of the highest openly advertised payout rates in the industry
GIA-certified gemologists on staff for accurate diamond and fine jewelry valuation
Thermo Fisher Scientific Niton XRF Analyzer used for non-destructive testing performed in front of the customer
9+ retail locations across Arizona and California with same-day payment at all locations
Nationwide mail-in service with free insured shipping kits for customers outside physical locations
Free, no-obligation appraisals — customer can walk away without selling
5.0 Google rating from 83 reviews; BBB-accredited (Tucson entity) since March 2022
cons: $50 assay/melt/treatment fee is applied to every lot, which meaningfully reduces net return on small or low-value transactions
All transactions are final — unlike a pawn shop, there is no redemption period to buy back sold items
Loan terms (listed as a service on their website) are not publicly disclosed, making that offering impossible to evaluate without contacting them
BBB accreditation is not consistent across all locations — the Scottsdale entity is listed as not BBB accredited
California presence is limited to the Inland Empire and San Diego areas; most locations are concentrated in Arizona
best_for: Estate executors or heirs liquidating inherited gold, silver jewelry, or flatware
Luxury watch owners looking to sell Rolex, Cartier, or other designer timepieces
Coin collectors and numismatists seeking specialist buyers for coins and bullion
Nationwide sellers of precious metals who want a mail-in option with insured shipping
diagnosis: PMR is best suited for sellers who have moderate-to-large quantities of precious metals, fine jewelry, or luxury watches and want transparent, specialist-driven valuations with same-day cash. The $50 per-lot assay fee makes small transactions less economical, so customers with a single low-value item may net more elsewhere. The published 94% gold payout rate provides a concrete benchmark that most competitors do not offer, making PMR a strong option for informed sellers who want to comparison-shop on a factual basis.
services: Buy gold — all karats including scrap, dental gold, and broken jewelry
Buy silver — coins, bars, bullion, and sterling flatware
Buy fine jewelry and loose diamonds
Buy luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe, and other designer brands)
Buy antiques, flatware, and estate contents
Nationwide mail-in service with free insured shipping kits
Free, no-obligation jewelry and precious metals appraisals
In-home consultations for large estate liquidations
